fact: June 27 is exactly 178 days into the year, which means there are only 187 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this date typically falls in the middle of peak firefly season - millions of tiny lights are blinking in backyards everywhere!
The sun sets super late around June 27 in places like Alaska - some towns get sunlight until almost midnight!
Baby sea turtles are often hatching on beaches around the world right now, using moonlight to find their way to the ocean for the first time.
June 27 falls in Cancer season, making it one of the most 'crabby' days of the year according to astrology!
Wild strawberries are usually at their juiciest and sweetest in forests across North America and Europe around this date.
The air temperature and water temperature in lakes are often almost exactly the same around June 27 in temperate regions - perfect for swimming!
Cicadas are typically screaming their loudest in many parts of the world right now, creating a buzzing symphony that can reach 120 decibels!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, June 27 marks the deep middle of winter - penguins are huddling for warmth!
The moon rises about 50 minutes later each night around this time of year, creating dramatically different evening skies from day to day.
Dandelions have usually transformed into those fluffy white puffballs by June 27, ready to spread their seeds with every gust of wind.
This date sits exactly one week after the summer solstice, meaning daylight is still incredibly long but starting its slow shrink toward winter.
